title: Mumbai Bhel Puri

subs.name: lightly crushed murmura can be replaced with unsweetened puffed wheat in a pinch, though texture differs

ingredients.name: potato (boiled, peeled, cut into 1 cm dice)
tamarind (seedless block, soaked and pulp extracted)

subs.name: 2 tablespoons ready-made tamarind paste from a jar; skip soaking, dissolve directly in 2 tablespoons water
an equal weight of brown sugar or a tablespoon of date syrup

steps.text: Place potatoes in a saucepan, cover with cold water and bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat and cook until a skewer slides through easily, about 12–15 minutes. Drain, cool slightly, peel and cut into 1 cm cubes. Keep aside.

Make the tamarind chutney: dissolve the soaked tamarind pulp in 4 tablespoons of warm water and strain through a sieve to remove fibres. Pour the strained liquid into a small saucepan, add jaggery, cumin seeds, and a pinch of salt. Simmer over low heat for 4–5 minutes, stirring until the jaggery melts and the chutney thickens to a loose syrup. Remove from heat and cool to room temperature.

Make the green chutney: blend the mint leaves, coriander leaves, one chopped green chilli, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt with 2–3 tablespoons of water until smooth. Taste and adjust salt or lemon. Transfer to a small bowl.

Place the puffed rice in a large wide bowl. Add the diced potato, chopped onion, chopped tomato, and the remaining chopped green chilli. Sprinkle over the chaat masala, amchur, and a little salt. Toss together gently with your hands or a spoon so the puffed rice stays light and airy.

Drizzle 2 tablespoons each of tamarind chutney and green chutney over the bhel mixture and toss to combine. Taste and add more chutney if you want it sweeter or more tangy. Scatter roasted peanuts and sev on top if using. Serve immediately on small plates or newspaper cones — bhel softens within 3–4 minutes of being dressed, so assemble just before eating.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tamarind → 2 tablespoons ready-made tamarind paste from a jar; skip soaking, dissolve directly in 2 tablespoons water; jaggery → an equal weight of brown sugar or a tablespoon of date syrup; puffed-rice → lightly crushed murmura can be replaced with unsweetened puffed wheat in a pinch, though texture differs
